name: Candy Cane Twist
recipeInstructions:
Dissolve yeast and 1 teaspoon sugar in water. Let sit until foamy, 5-10 minutes.
Place half of hazelnuts in a food processor; process until chopped. Remove and reserve.
Place remaining nuts in the processor along with remaining sugar. Process until finely ground.
Add flour, salt, and butter to the processor. With the machine running, add egg and yeast mixture.
Process until the dough forms a ball. Continue processing for 30 seconds more to knead.
Transfer dough to a large greased bowl; turn to coat. Cover with a damp cloth. Let rise in a warm place, away from drafts, until doubled in bulk, about 1½ hours.
Punch dough down. On a lightly floured surface, combine remaining nuts and dried cherries or raisins. Divide the dough into two halves.
Roll each half into a 24-inch long rope. Twist the ropes together.
On a greased sheet, form the braided loaf into a cane shape. Cover with a damp cloth. Let rise in a warm place, away from drafts, until doubled in bulk, about 1 hour.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Bake for 25-30 minutes or until golden brown and hollow sounding when tapped. Cool bread on a wire rack.
Prepare icing: Stir together 1½ cups confectioners sugar and about 2-3 tablespoons milk until blended and of good icing consistency.
NOTE: To toast hazelnuts, spread nuts on a baking sheet. Bake in the preheated oven for 8-10 minutes or until toasted. Rub nuts in a clean kitchen towel to remove the skins.
